records.ety: [OE. arayngen, OF. arengier, F. arranger, fr. a (L. ad) + OF. rengier, rangier, F. ranger. See Range, v. t.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Arranged; p. pr. & vb. n. Arranging (<?/).]

defs: To put in proper order; to dispose (persons, or parts) in the manner intended, or best suited for the purpose; as, troops arranged for battle.
quotes: So [they] came to the market place, and there he arranged his men in the streets.
[They] were beginning to arrange their hampers.

defs: To adjust or settle; to prepare; to determine; as, to arrange the preliminaries of an undertaking.
